title: 2 Terminology
body_text:
2	Terminology
2.1	Definitions
Batch
A section of a file consisting of an A record, detail records and a Z record. A file can consist of more than one batch.
Business Day
A day that isnât a weekend or statutory holiday.
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1âs applications and to information and news about our products and services.
Compressed Format
A field format in which leading spaces, trailing spaces, and blank fields may be omitted to save space.
File and Report Exchange
The File and Report Exchange allows authorized users to download files from Central 1 and upload files to Central 1. Users can connect to the File and Report Exchange via direct connection (SFTP) or online via the Client Centre.
Header Record
The record preceding detail records, indicating the start of a batch and containing general information that applies to all the subsequent detail records.
Merchant
A retailer who provides VISA services to clients.
Merchant Transaction
In the VISA and EFT/POS services, the side of the transaction that is posted to the merchantâs account at its financial institution.
Record
A group of fields containing information about one item. The âitemâ can be a transaction, a member, an account, and so on.
Statutory Holiday
For the purposes of the production of files, the following are recognized as Canadian statutory holidays:
New Yearâs Day, or the day it is observed
Good Friday
Victoria Day
Canada Day, or the day it is observed
Labour Day
National Day for Truth and Reconciliation, or the day it is observed
Thanksgiving Day
Remembrance Day, or the day it is observed
Christmas Day, or the day it is observed
Boxing Day, or the day it is observed
In the case of USD transaction files, US statutory holidays could also be observed.
Trailer Record
The record following detail records, indicating the end of a batch and containing summary information that applies to all the preceding detail records.
END
